---
title: "Backends"
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
toc-location: left
---

`saguiSED` is a lightweight R interface around optional SED-fitting backends.
The package prepares regional photometry, writes backend inputs, collects fit
summaries, and paints fitted quantities back onto the segmentation map.

## Bagpipes

The current backend is [Bagpipes](https://bagpipes.readthedocs.io/en/latest/).
Bagpipes is developed independently of `saguiSED`; this package only provides a
small bridge from region-integrated photometric SED tables to the Bagpipes
Python interface.

Install and cite Bagpipes following the
[official Bagpipes documentation](https://bagpipes.readthedocs.io/en/latest/).

```r
check_bagpipes()
```

## Model helper

`bagpipes_model()` stores the compact set of choices currently exposed by
`saguiSED`.

```r
model <- bagpipes_model(
  sfh = "exponential",
  dust = "calzetti",
  metallicity = "free",
  systematic_frac = 0.10
)
```

The helper is intentionally simple. For full Bagpipes model flexibility, use
Bagpipes directly and pass `saguiSED` regional photometry as input.

## Fitting

```r
fit <- fit_region_seds(
  sed,
  backend = "bagpipes",
  model = model,
  redshift = 1.10,
  out_dir = "bagpipes_region_fits"
)
```

The backend writes a self-contained output folder containing:

- `regional_photometry_input.csv`
- `filter_set.csv`
- `region_fit_summary.csv`
- `model_photometry.csv`
- `model_spectrum.csv`, when returned by the backend
- per-region diagnostic plots generated by the backend

`saguiSED` then reads these files into a `sagui_sed_fit` object.

## What belongs here

`saguiSED` is for SED fitting of region-integrated photometric measurements.
It does not implement IFU spectral fitting. Spectroscopy-focused fitting should
live in `capivara` or a capivara-specific extension, where the data model and
scientific assumptions are different.

## Backend roadmap

The backend layer is meant to be replaceable. Bagpipes is the first backend
because it is widely used and naturally supports photometric SED fitting. Future
backends can follow the same pattern:

- read a `sagui_sed_table`
- write backend-specific inputs
- return a `sagui_sed_fit` object
- expose comparable summary columns for map painting

This keeps the core `sagui` segmentation package small and lets SED-fitting
dependencies evolve independently.
